claim: PCF-1 v1.3 §6 Theorem 5 (TeX line 525) defines A as the coefficient of -n log n in the asymptotic expansion log|delta_n| = -A n log n + alpha n - beta_w log n + gamma_w + sum_k h_k/n^k as n -> infty, with empirical record A in {3,4} for d=2 PCFs.

PCF-1 v1.3 §5 Spec(K) framework (TeX line ~225) defines the d=k balanced class as the leading-degree pair (deg a, deg b) = (k+1, k) in the delta-direction and (k-1, k) in the alpha-direction; for d=2 this gives deg b_n = 2, deg a_n = 1.

Birkhoff & Trjitzinsky 1933 §1 (Acta Math 60, page 4) gives the canonical formal solution at infinity as y_hat(x) = exp(Q(x)) s(x) with Q(x) = (mu/p) x log x + gamma x + delta_1 x^{(p-1)/p} + ... + delta_{p-1} x^{1/p}; in the normal case p=1 this reduces to Q_j(x) = mu_j x log x + gamma_j x.

Birkhoff & Trjitzinsky 1933 §1 (Acta Math 60, page 5, footnote 2) cites C. R. Adams, On the Irregular Cases of the Linear Ordinary Difference Equations, Trans. Am. Math. Soc., vol. 30 (1928), pp. 507-541, and characterises Adams 1928 in the page-5 main text as treating the irregular normal case using methods continuous with Birkhoff's prior work.
source: bt1933 §1 (Acta Math 60, p.5 = PDF page 5, footnote 2)

claim: Birkhoff & Trjitzinsky 1933 §4 Lemma 8 (Acta Math 60, page 30) takes as input H(x) = exp(Q(x)) h(x) with Q(x) = mu x log x + gamma x + ... + nu x^v of the same canonical form as §1, recording that §§4-6 inherit the §1 normalisation without re-definition.
source: bt1933 §4 Lemma 8 (Acta Math 60, p.30 = PDF page 30)

claim: Birkhoff 1930 (Acta Math 54, page 2) characterises Adams 1928 formal series as having form x^{mu*} exp(gamma x) (a + b/x + ...) with mu* <= mu, and explicitly notes they 'are of the same kind as appear in the regular case', i.e. share the Birkhoff 1930 normalisation.

Birkhoff 1930 §1 (Acta Math 54, page 6, formula (6)) gives the canonical formal solution s(x) = x^{nu x} exp(P(x)) x^{mu} (a + b/x^{1/p} + ...) with P(x) = gamma x + delta x^{(p-1)/p} + ..., and the leading-balance Newton-polygon slope formula mu = (j_l - j_m)/(l - m) where a_i(x) ~ a_{ij_i} x^{-j_i/p}.
source: birkhoff_1930 §1 (Acta Math 54, p.6 = PDF page 6, formula (6))

claim: Wasow 1965 chapter X (Dover reprint, 04_wasow_1965_dover.pdf, 5.4 MB, 89 pages) is on disk but contains no OCR text layer; pypdf 6.10.2 extracts 773 bytes total across 89 pages (only page-header markers). Therefore Wasow §X.2-§X.3 cannot be primary-quoted in this session; the agent triangulates through Birkhoff 1930 + B-T 1933 directly.
source: wasow_1965 ch.X (Dover reprint, image-only PDF, NIA)

claim: Triangulation table (D5) records that Birkhoff 1930 nu, B-T 1933 mu, Adams 1928 sigma (per transitive citation), and SIARC PCF-1 v1.3 A all denote the same coefficient of x log x (resp. n log n) in the canonical formal-solution exponent at infinity; there is no factor of 2 between these conventions at the normalisation level. The Phase-1 worry sigma_Wasow = 2 sigma_Adams is identified as a paraphrase artefact and is not supported by on-disk primary evidence.
source: triangulation across bt1933 + birkhoff_1930 + pcf1_v13 (D1+D3+D4 verbatim quotes)

claim: Verdict A01_WASOW_READING_CONFIRMED: the Phase-1 literature-derivable bracket A in [d, 2d] for d>=3 is supported under the Wasow-normalisation reading; the alternative Adams-normalisation reading (which would shift the bracket to [d/2, d] and place B4 = 2d outside the literature bracket) is not supported by Birkhoff 1930 page 2 + B-T 1933 page 5 direct citations of Adams 1928 with same convention.
source: verdict synthesis from D5 triangulation table
